content: We can frame naturally occurring biological agents (such as invasive microbial pathogens spreading through new ecosystems) as unoptimized swarm agents that follow an implicit, evolved version of the 2025 configurable ICM curiosity mechanism, where intrinsic reward for exploring novel uncolonized niches drives their collective behavior. This perspective lets us adapt 2025 ICM frameworks for swarm UAVs to predict and model the spread of biological agents in new, partially mapped environments.
